title: HERODIAS Daughter presenting to her Mother St. JOHN's Head in a Charger, also Painted by her self by Anne Killigrew — Love Poetry Lines
meaning: "HERODIAS Daughter presenting to her Mother St. JOHN's Head in a Charger, also Painted by her self" by Anne Killigrew is a love and inspirational poem consisting of 13 lines. The poet explores themes of love and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "BEhold, dear Mother, who was late our Fear, Disarm'd and Harmless, I present you here;...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Anne Killigrew's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
